title: acaena magellanica
intro: Acaena magellanica, also known as the New Zealand thyme or red buttons, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae, characterized by its red or purple berries and finely dissected leaves. Acaena magellanica has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its high content of tannins and other bioactive compounds. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and digestive problems, due to its astringent, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. Acaena magellanica is typically prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in topical applications, such as creams and ointments, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
